Table 3: Fine-grained class-level continual learning results. † denotes replay-based variants. Results are averaged over five random seeds.

Table 6: Summary of the hierarchical continual MAS evaluation protocol.

Figure 2: Overview of MasFACT. The framework has three stages: historical topology construction (Sec. 3.1), geometry-aware prior retrieval (Sec. 3.2), and conservative posterior adaptation (Sec. 3.3).
Table 11: Domain- and task-level continual learning results with standard deviations. † denotes replay-based variants. Results are averaged over five random seeds.

Table 4: Plug-and-play generalization of MasFACT across MAS frameworks. Green arrows report absolute gains for AA and absolute reductions for AF.
Figure 3: Mechanistic analysis of structural forgetting. Left: topology drift tracks old-task accuracy degradation. Right: forgetting increases as the retrieved prior becomes more distant.
